pg132-p1-s2:
If he does not argue about religion, he will make new friends and is sure to find new
avenues of usefulness and pleasure.
pg132-p1-s3: He and his family can be a bright spot in such congregations.
pg132-p1-s4:

and new courage to many a priest, minister, or rabbi, who gives his all to minister to our troubled world.

pg132-p1-s6: So far as we are concerned, there is nothing obligatory about it.
pg132-p1-s7:

pg132-p1-s8: Each individual should consult his own conscience.
pg132-p1:
differ with them on many matters. If he does not argue about religion, he will make new friends and is sure to find new
avenues of usefulness and pleasure. He and his family can be a bright spot in such congregations. He may bring new hope
and new courage to many a priest, minister, or rabbi, who gives his all to minister to our troubled world. We intend the
foregoing as a helpful suggestion only. So far as we are concerned, there is nothing obligatory about it. As
non-denominational people, we cannot make up others' minds for them. Each individual should consult his own conscience.
pg132-p2-s1: We have been speaking to you of serious, sometimes tragic things.
pg132-p2-s2: We have been dealing with alcohol in its worst aspect.
pg132-p2-s3: But we aren't a glum lot.
pg132-p2-s4: If newcomers could see no joy or fun in our existence, they wouldn't want it.
pg132-p2-s5:
We absolutely
insist on enjoying life.
pg132-p2-s6:
We try not to indulge in cynicism over the state of the nations, nor do we carry the world's
troubles on our shoulders.
pg132-p2-s7:
When we see a man sinking into the mire that is alcoholism, we give him first aid and place
what we have at his disposal.
pg132-p2-s8: For his sake, we do recount and almost relive the horrors of our past.
pg132-p2-s9:
But those of us who
have tried to shoulder the entire burden and trouble of others find we are soon overcome by them.
pg132-p2:
We have been speaking to you of serious, sometimes tragic things. We have been dealing with alcohol in its worst aspect. But we aren't a glum lot. If newcomers could see no joy or fun in our existence, they wouldn't want it. We absolutely
insist on enjoying life. We try not to indulge in cynicism over the state of the nations, nor do we carry the world's
troubles on our shoulders. When we see a man sinking into the mire that is alcoholism, we give him first aid and place
what we have at his disposal. For his sake, we do recount and almost relive the horrors of our past. But those of us who
have tried to shoulder the entire burden and trouble of others find we are soon overcome by them.
pg132-p3-s1: So we think cheerfulness and laughter make for usefulness.
pg132-p3-s2:
Outsiders are sometimes shocked when we burst into merriment
over a seemingly tragic experience out of the past.
pg132-p3-s4:
We have recovered, and have been given
the power to help others.
pg132-p3:
So we think cheerfulness and laughter make for usefulness. Outsiders are sometimes shocked when we burst into merriment
over a seemingly tragic experience out of the past. But why shouldn't we laugh? We have recovered, and have been given
the power to help others.
pg132-p4-s1: Everybody knows that those in bad health, and those who seldom play, do not laugh much.
